best.explanation: Belgas looks like a small north coast window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 35 kJ, 12 s period, lully 3/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 10-13s at mid tide also works on Belgas, but low tide is not a deal-breaker, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot.
best.shortExplanation: Belgas is an open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything · label Rare window, Low confidence · 0.3-0.6 m · 35 kJ · 12 s period · 10-13s at mid tide also works on Belgas, but low tide is not a deal-breaker · wind is the main risk · Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Almagreira looks like a small north coast window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 28 kJ, 5 s period, lully 1/100, and wind is the main risk.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot.
ranked.shortExplanation: Almagreira is an exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal · label Rare window, Low confidence · 0.3-0.6 m · 28 kJ · 5 s period · 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable · wind is the main risk · Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot.

ranked.explanation: Lagide looks like a fun-sized window for Fun progression, Low confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 49 kJ, 12 s period, lully 10/100, and wind is the main risk.  A dependable left-hand reef that turns modest swell into real rides; this is best for clean, playful walls when the bigger beaches are too wild or too small; 10-13s at high tide is softer but still workable on the reef shelves.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot; gusts over 35 km/h could make it messy.

ranked.explanation: Supertubos looks like a clean small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, 14 kJ, 12 s period, lully 0/100, and good wind at 20 km/h.  A fast, hollow sand-bottom wave that gets serious quickly; this is best for short, punchy bodyboard barrels when it is small enough for comfort; 10-13s at mid tide is the prime barrel / wedge setup for Supertubos / Molhe Leste.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; Supertubos looks below your bodyboard fun size.
